LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DARLENE ANAYA'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS' ENTIRE COST MEMORANDUM, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO TAX IS GRANTED. BASED ON THE COURT'S FAMILIARITY WITH THIS CASE, THE COURT HAS DETERMINED THAT, WHILE SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS PROPERLY GRANTED IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANTS, THE CLAIMS ASSERTED BY MS. ANAYA WERE NOT OBJECTIVELY GROUNDLESS AND THUS DEFENDANTS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF COSTS PER WILLIAMS V. CHINO VALLEY INDEPENDENT FIRE DISTRICT (2015) 61 CAL. 4TH 97. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Strike Defendants' Entire Cost Memorandum, Or In The Alternative, To Tax
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF DARLENE ANAYA's MOTION To Strike Defendants' Entire Cost Memorandum, Or In The Alternative, To Tax is GRANTED. Based on the court's familiarity with this case, the court has determined that, while summary judgment was properly granted in favor of the defendants, the claims asserted by Ms. Anaya were not objectively groundless and thus defendants are not entitled to an award of costs per Williams v. Chino Valley Independent Fire District (2015) 61 Cal. 4th 97. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Defendant'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Business Records And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants United Educators of San Francisco and Dennis Kelly's motion to quash deposition subpoenas and request for monetary sanctions is taken off calendar as to the motion to quash and denied as to the request for monetary sanctions. Plaintiff Darlene Anaya's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. The motion to quash is moot by the granting of defendants' motion for summary judgment. Each side had some substantial justification for her or their position, so monetary sanctions is unwarranted. Order signed. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant Uesf
Off Calendar Dec-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Edward M. Anaya of the law offices of Anaya Law (415.300.0871) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Jannah Manasala of the law offices of Weinberg Roger & Rosenfeld (510.337.1001) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Both sides stipulate to the judge pro tempore. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides in open court. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification. On the Court's direction the parties meet, confer and agree on the laguage of the proposed written order.

PLAINTIFF DARLENE ANAYA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant UESF is GRANTED in part. Given the liberal policy favoring discovery and the broad definition of relevance to the subject matter, Motion to Compel granted subject to the terms of the protective order and limited to the time frame of May 2011 through June 2016 subject to any appropriate redactions based on privacy rights of third parties or other privileged information. No sanctions awarded to either party.

Furthermore, the parties agree that this order is only in regards to the general potential relevancy of the discovery sought. Whether or not any information stemming from the granting of this motion is admissible is not being decided. The parties can debate the admissibility of this information at a later date.

Furthermore, the parties agree that this order is limited to defendant UESF's documents, and does not apply to documents created by SFUSD. Defendant UESF is therefore ordered to produce all documents created by it or its agents from May 2011 to June 2016 regarding any analysis of documents collected by defendant UESF or its agents under Article VI of its Bylaws.

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert F. Kane; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)
